channel.title: Morning Score
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Score is your guide to the year-round campaign cycle.

item.title: Dems embrace Platner, mostly, while GOP goes on attack
item.description: Rather than explicitly praise Platner, national Democrats broadly focused more on slamming GOP Sen. Susan Collins.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Unpacking establishment Dems’ wishcasting scenarios to avoid being stuck with Platner
item.description: Maine oysterman Graham Platner is all but sure to win his Democratic primary to face Sen. Susan Collins on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Lessons from a jam-packed primary night
item.dc:creator: By Lisa Kashinsky and Andrew Howard
item.dc:contributor: By Lisa Kashinsky and Andrew Howard

item.title: Iowa’s flood of primary cash
item.description: VoteVets' spending in the Democratic Senate primary has become so dominant that it’s taken on a life of its own on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Lone Star takeaways
item.description: Three takeaways from Tuesday's Texas runoff results.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Crockett’s lasting influence in two Texas runoffs
item.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D-Texas) isn’t on the ballot today, after she lost her March Senate primary to state Rep. James Talarico. But her influence in the Lone Star state — especially in heavily-Black deep-blue areas — is as clear as ever.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: MAGA’s reign continues
item.description: November, however, is shaping up to be a different story.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: The only certainty about Georgia’s elections tomorrow
item.description: Several of Georgia’s marquee races remain unusually difficult to predict heading into Tuesday, underscoring the volatile political dynamics — and exhausted electorate — in one of the nation’s most important battlegrounds.
item.dc:creator: By Erin Doherty, Alec Hernandez and Andrew Howard
item.dc:contributor: By Erin Doherty, Alec Hernandez and Andrew Howard

item.title: Tomorrow’s weirdest primary
item.description: The Nebraska Democratic Party is boosting a candidate in tomorrow’s Senate primary who hopes to never appear on November’s ballot.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Redistricting will continue until morale improves
item.description: Eight states have already enacted new maps ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard

item.title: Republicans blame Dems after WHCD
item.description: Republicans are using a playbook they honed after the assassination attempts against Donald Trump
item.dc:creator: By Lisa Kashinsky

item.title: Pennsylvania's Mastriano mayhem
item.description: A campaign to write in Doug Mastriano for Pennsylvania governor is worrying some state Republicans.
item.dc:creator: By Lisa Kashinsky

item.title: Latinos have turned on Trump — but Dems haven't closed the deal
item.description: Just 30 percent of the 1,600 Latino likely voters surveyed across eight battleground states approved of Trump’s handling of the economy.
item.dc:creator: By Andrew Howard
